entry.title: Expiring HTLCs without free relay
entry.summary: The concept discussed revolves around a mechanism in network transactions where the necessity of free relay is eliminated, thus providing a notable advantage. The key aspect of this approach is that it does not prevent replacement cycling within the refund path, unlike mechanisms such as `OP_EXPIRE`. Instead, it strategically removes the economic incentive for attacks by ensuring an eventual refund if the preimage is not published promptly. This system ensures that either the routing node can claim the inbound funds within a sufficient time frame or they are assured a refund. This method provides a more secure and reliable framework for handling transactions, especially in scenarios prone to delays or non-compliance.
